_comment: Grammar scar regex catalog for verify-all-decks.mjs Check #25.
_howToAdd: Add new broken patterns as they are discovered in batch content rewrites. Each entry has a pattern (literal string) and a human-readable description of what caused it.
_note: Patterns must be specific enough to avoid false positives. 'the concept of' and 'the concept that' are valid English and must NOT be included as patterns. Use patterns like ' a the ' (double article) or 'X-the concept' (hyphenated) or 'the concept Y' (noun following without 'of'/'that') rather than just 'the concept '.

patterns.description: 'a the' double article — caused by naive word-substitution inserting 'a' before a phrase that already begins with 'the'. Example: 'bound to a the reactant molecule'.
patterns.example: What forms when a the reactant molecule binds the active site?

patterns.description: 'the the' double definite article — caused by inserting 'the concept' (which starts with 'the') after a word that also ends with 'the'. Example: 'also called the the concept threshold'.
patterns.example: also called the the concept threshold — is known by what abbreviation?

patterns.description: Hyphenated 'X-the concept' — caused by replacing one part of a compound noun (e.g. 'logic-programming') with 'the concept', leaving the hyphen. Example: 'logic-the concept approach'.
patterns.example: reflecting its logic-the concept approach where you declare facts

patterns.description: 'encoding the concept that assigns' — specific broken pattern where 'encoding' is the category and 'the concept' was erroneously inserted before 'that assigns'.
patterns.example: the 1963 7-bit character encoding the concept that assigns numeric codes

patterns.description: 'the this' double determiner — caused by inserting 'this' after a phrase that already ends with 'the'. Example: 'in the this context'.
patterns.example: Which element is found in the this compound?

patterns.description: Proper noun + 'this' — caused by replacing a proper noun (e.g., 'Union') with the placeholder 'this' without removing the preceding adjective. Example: 'the Soviet this collapsed in 1991'.
patterns.example: When did the Soviet this collapse?

patterns.description: 'in a who' — caused by replacing a person/entity noun with 'who' inside a prepositional phrase. Example: 'studied in a who discovered...'.
patterns.example: This technique was developed in a who pioneered...

patterns.description: 'anatomical this' — caused by replacing an anatomical noun with 'this' while leaving the preceding adjective. Example: 'the anatomical this connects...'.
patterns.example: The anatomical this connects two chambers.

patterns.description: 'UN this introduced' — caused by replacing 'report' or 'document' with 'this' while leaving 'UN'. Example: 'What 1987 UN this introduced the concept...'.
patterns.example: What 1987 UN this introduced the concept of sustainable development?
